questions.clue: In 1904's election Alton B. Parker & 80-year-old running mate Henry Davis went down to defeat against this pres.
The first invasion with a main role for these troops was when the Nazi Fallschirmjäger descended from the sky on Crete
In 1941 Abe Reles, an informer on this homicidal "corporation", went down in history—6 stories straight down
In 1886 PM Gladstone suffered defeat when he tried to get home rule for this island
The documentary "The Battle of Chile" chronicles events leading to this politician's downfall
Sydney, Higgins & Big Ears are among the koala mascots used by this airline over the years
Misha, the mascot for the 1980 Summer Olympics in Moscow, was this type of animal
In 2011 thieves in Ireland made off with a statue of Mr. Bib, this tire company's mascot
Originally named Jumpman, this Nintendo mascot got a new name for Donkey Kong Jr.
A Berkeley blog likened Stanford's unofficial mascot, one of these, to a "constipated squid struggling with dinner"

categories.name: THE NEW YORK TIMES PULITZER WINNERS

questions.clue: (I'm David Barstow.) My Pulitzer-winning story about retired generals co-opted as analysts to sell the Iraq War was headlined this building's "Hidden Hand"
(I'm Nicholas Kristof.) In 2006 I won a Pulitzer Prize for commentary that raised public awareness of the genocide in this region of Sudan
(I'm Linda Greenhouse.) I won a 1998 Pulitzer for covering the Supreme Court with articles like "Why" this name of a failed nominee "Is Still a Verb in Politics 10 Years Later"
(Andrea Elliott delivers the clue.) Chronicling the life of an immigrant in Brooklyn who is one of these Muslim leaders of a mosque earned me a 2007 Pulitzer Prize
(I'm Carolyn Ryan.) In 2009 I was part of the team that won the Pulitzer for breaking news coverage of this New York governor & his stunning fall from grace

dailyDouble.clue: Cotton is a traditional fabric for this activity of making blankets into colorful art
